params.text:
Common causes at work

Most slips happen when a floor becomes wet or contaminated, and most trips are caused by poor housekeeping – clutter and obstructions left in walkways. Understanding the usual causes helps you spot a hazard before it causes harm.

params.title: Tap a card to flip it
params.description: Common workplace slip and trip hazards and how they arise.

dialogs.answer: Spilt drinks, cleaning fluids, rainwater tracked in at doorways or bodily fluids all reduce grip. Clean up straight away and use a warning sign until the floor is dry.
Leads from hoists, chargers, vacuum cleaners and medical equipment stretched across walkways are a classic trip hazard. Route cables around the edge or use covers.
Boxes, laundry, mobility aids and rubbish left in corridors block clear routes. Good housekeeping – a place for everything – is the simplest prevention.
Dim corridors, stairwells and bulbs that have blown make hazards hard to see. Report failed lighting promptly so it can be replaced.
Worn soles, backless shoes or high heels give poor grip and stability. Wear well-fitting, flat, non-slip shoes suited to the work.
Loose carpet, curled mat edges, worn thresholds and cracked paving cause trips. Report defects so they can be repaired.
